news.summary: A significant milestone has been achieved with the initial analysis of data from the OBOE study conducted across four sites. A key member of this study is a specialist from Cincinnati Children's, serving as a co-first author. This development comes in the context of a worrisome trend where pregnant women are using opioids, including.
news.headline: Opioid use during pregnancy linked to smaller newborn brains in study of 4 sites, led by Cincinnati expert.

news.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) is set to unveil its first-quarter financial results on April 11. Analysts predict earnings per share to reach $4.64, a jump from $4.44 in the same period last year. The bank is expected to post quarterly revenue of $44.14 billion, compared to $41.93 billion a year ago. CEO Jamie Dimon cautioned about the increased recession risk due to U.S.-China trade tensions following President Trump's tariff policies. The company's annual dividend yield stands at 2.66%, with a quarterly dividend of $1.40 per share ($5.60 annually), attracting investor attention. For more details, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: JPMorgan Q1 earnings predicted at $4.64/share, revenue of $44.14B. CEO Dimon warns of risks from trade tensions. Annual dividend yield of 2.66%.

news.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSM) recently disclosed a significant surge in revenue for the first quarter, with a growth rate of 41.6% year-on-year, reaching 839.25 billion New Taiwan dollars (equivalent to $25.5 billion). C.C. Wei, the company's key executive, outlined plans in March to invest an extra $100 billion in U.S. chipmaking, in addition to the previously allocated $65 billion by April 2024. This decision was influenced by criticism from President Trump, who accused Taiwan of "stealing" the U.S. semiconductor business and causing substantial trade deficits for Washington. For further details on this development, please refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Taiwan Semiconductor Q1 revenue up 41.6% to $25.5 billion, pledges $100 billion to US chipmaking amidst tensions with Trump.

news.summary: Economists and policymakers caution that the most recent data showing a temporary improvement may not last, with tariffs potentially leading to increased inflation.
news.headline: In March, inflation slightly reduced, but tariffs may push prices higher soon, warn experts.

news.summary: Kansas City highlights its status as a prominent design hub by hosting a total of 25 engaging events starting on Earth Day, April 22. The events are open to the public, and tickets can be purchased at kcdesignweek.org. KC Design Week features a mix of both complimentary and paid events to cater to varying interests. Taking place in Kansas City, Missouri, this initiative showcases the city's creative prowess and commitment to innovative design practices.
news.headline: Kansas City's Center for Architecture & Design hosts KC Design Week with 25 events starting on Earth Day, April 22. Tickets at kcdesignweek.org.

news.summary: In March, there was a notable decrease in price pressures, providing significant relief amid concerns about the impact of trade tariffs. This resulted in a boost in investor sentiment following President Donald Trump's announcement of a 90-day tariff pause. The annual inflation rate dropped to 2.4%, its lowest level in six months, down from 2.8% in February, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This figure was lower than the anticipated 2.6%. Monthly, the Consumer Price Index saw a 0.1% contraction, a slowdown from the 0.2% increase in the previous month and below the expected 0.1% rise. This reflects the lowest monthly inflation rate since May 2020. The decrease was primarily driven by a 6.3% decline in gasoline prices, although this was partly offset by rises in electricity and natural gas indexes. Conversely, the food index increased by 0.4% in March.
news.headline: March inflation rate falls to 2.4%, lowest in 6 months, easing concerns over tariff effects, raising hopes for Fed rate cuts.

news.summary: Brunswick Corporation (NYSE: BC) will release its 2025 first quarter financial results on April 24, 2025. The results will be available on the Company's website and the SEC's website. A conference call at 10 a.m. CT/ 11 a.m. ET on April 24, 2025, will be hosted by David M. Foulkes, CEO, and Ryan M. Gwillim, CFO and Chief Strategy Officer. Security analysts and investors can participate via telephone or internet. A replay of the call will be available until May 1, 2025. Brunswick Corporation is the global leader in marine recreation. For more information, visit https://ir.brunswick.com.
news.headline: Brunswick Corp to release 2025 Q1 results on April 24, 2025, followed by earnings call at 10am CT/11am ET. CEO and CFO to host, call details provided. About Brunswick Corp: NYSE: BC, leader in marine recreation. Find full story on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: NTIC reported their financial results for the second quarter of fiscal 2025 with key numbers as follows: Consolidated net sales decreased 8.5% to $19,072,000. ZERUST™ industrial net sales decreased 3.7% to $12,562,000. ZERUST™ oil and gas net sales decreased 28.5% to $1,549,000. Natur-Tec™ product net sales decreased 11.8% to $4,960,000. NTIC China net sales increased 8.1% to $3,735,000. Gross profit decreased to 35.6%. Joint venture operating income decreased 31.8% to $1,691,000, and operating expenses increased to $8,820,000. Net income attributable to NTIC was $434,000, with earnings per diluted share of $0.04. Cash provided by operating activities was $3,199,000 for the six months ended February 28, 2025. NTIC believes in a rebound in sales for Natur-Tec™ and ZERUST™ oil and gas in the second half of the fiscal year. The company is focusing on cash management and debt reduction. NTIC's consolidated net sales decreased 8.5% to $19,072,000 during the three months ended February 28, 2025. NTIC had working capital of $21,416,000 as of February 28, 2025, including $5,091,000 in cash. Joint venture operating income decreased 31.8% to $1,691,000 during the three months ended February 28, 2025. Operating expenses were 46.2% of net sales for the second quarter of fiscal 2025. Net income and diluted earnings per share were $434,000 and $0.04, respectively. NTIC will host a conference call to discuss its results and outlook. They develop corrosion prevention products under ZERUST™ and bio-based polymer compounds under Natur-Tec™. The company faces risks related to the global economy, competition, regulatory changes, and intellectual property. Non-GAAP financial measures are provided in addition to GAAP measures. NTIC's consolidated balance sheet shows current assets at $39,624,812 and property & equipment net at $14,814,389 as of February 28, 2025. Investments in joint ventures were at $25,041,302. More detailed information is available on the company's website and in its SEC filings.
news.headline: NTIC reported Q2 fiscal 2025 financial results: net sales decreased 8.5% to $19,072,000. ZERUST industrial net sales decreased 3.7% to $12,562,000, oil and gas net sales decreased 28.5% to $1,549,000, Natur-Tec product net sales decreased 11.8% to $4,960,000. NTIC China net sales increased 8.1% to $3,735,000. Operating income decreased 31.8% to $1,691,000. Net income was $434,000 compared to $1,701,000. Operating expenses increased 2.4% to $8,820,000. Cash provided by operating activities was $3,199,000 for the six months ended February 28, 2025. CEO Patrick Lynch expects a rebound in sales in the second half of the year. Non-GAAP adjusted net income for Q2 was a loss of $300,000. NTIC's working capital was $21,416,000 as of February 28, 2025.

news.summary: Sucro Limited, a sugar refiner focusing on North American markets, reported impressive financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024. Sugar deliveries in Q4 increased by 61% compared to the prior year, with refining operations seeing a 30% volume growth. Quarterly revenue reached $160.4 million with 154,773 metric tons delivered. Full-year revenue totaled $654.4 million from 649,747 metric tons. Net income for the year was $24.2 million, with a Q4 net loss of $6.9 million. Adjusted gross profit for the year was $6.5 million with a margin of 8.6%, and for Q4 it was $12.4 million with a margin of 7.7%. EBITDA for the full year was $61.7 million, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $35.7 million. In Q4, EBITDA was $1.5 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $8.5 million. Adjusted gross profit per metric ton delivered for the year was $80.07, and for Q4 it was $87.05. Refinery volumes for the year were 206,994 metric tons, with Q4 seeing 44,534 metric tons. The Adjusted gross profit per metric ton delivered for refineries was $140.56 for the year and $146.08 for Q4.Revenue for the full year was $654,422 with a 31.7% growth from the previous year. The gross profit for the year was 85,202, increasing by 20.4%, whereas the Q4 gross profit was 5,847, increasing significantly by 233.9% compared to the prior year. Net income for the year was 24,191, rising by 21.1%. Overall, Sucro Limited showed strong growth and financial performance in Q4 and throughout 2024.
news.headline: Sucro Limited reported a 61.4% increase in Q4 sugar deliveries, reaching 154,773 metric tons. Full-year revenue hit $654.4 million with 649,747 metric tons delivered. Net income was $24.2 million for the year. Adjusted EBITDA for 2024 was $35.7 million. Refineries saw volumes of 206,994 metric tons for the year. Q4 revenue reached $160.4 million. Full text on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Confidence among North America-based accountants has dropped to its lowest level on record, according to the latest quarterly Global Economic Conditions Survey (GECS) conducted by ACCA and IMA. The survey, released from Washington and Montvale, N.J. on April 10, 2025, revealed a concerning trend as global accountant confidence decreased for the third consecutive quarter. This decline signifies a significant loss in optimism within the industry, highlighting the challenges faced by accountants in the current economic climate. The data presents a stark picture, pointing toward underlying concerns that have impacted professionals in the sector.
news.headline: Q1 2025 sees a sharp decline in U.S., lowest confidence level among North American accountants - source: GECS.

news.headline: Bitcoin and Ethereum prices surge as inflation sharply decreases in March, raising concerns about tariffs impact.

news.summary: Renowned economist Peter Schiff criticized President Trump's tariff policy, comparing it to the catastrophic Hindenburg airship disaster of 1937. Schiff argued that Trump's aggressive tariff threats have weakened the U.S. economy and reduced his negotiating power on the global stage. The sudden pause in implementing tariffs may have shown trading partners that Trump's threats are not credible, potentially compromising future trade negotiations. Schiff described Trump's tariff strategy as highly flawed and likened it to the Hindenburg disaster, suggesting that it marked a tragic end to an era. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Renowned economist Peter Schiff criticizes Trump's tariff policy, comparing it to the Hindenburg disaster. Schiff says Trump diminished his negotiating power by implementing and pausing tariffs abruptly. Trump is called a "paper tiger" for his flawed tactics.

news.summary: A devastating earthquake hit Myanmar near Mandalay on March 28 with a powerful 7.7 magnitude, leading to over 3,000 confirmed deaths and an increasing count of injured and missing individuals. The impact of the tragedy is severe, with a growing number of casualties.
news.headline: Global Catalytic Ministries saved 3,000 lives in Afghanistan & now mobilizing for Myanmar, where 7.7 quake left over 3,000 dead.

news.summary: In March, there was a significant surge in global supply chains' spare capacity, reaching the highest level seen since May 2020 during the peak of the COVID-19 crisis. This surge indicates a rapid decline in conditions for manufacturers worldwide. Factories in the United States, Mexico, and Canada experienced a sharp pullback in March, likely due to tariffs. This decline in manufacturing was particularly pronounced due to the challenging conditions, with the number of affected factories being significant in these countries.
news.headline: North American manufacturers see sharp decline due to tariffs, while Asian suppliers operate at full capacity: GEP Global Supply Chain Volatility Index. Global supply chain capacity highest since May 2020; US, Mexico, Canada factories retreat.

news.summary: In a recent announcement, Biocon Biologics Ltd (BBL), a subsidiary of Biocon Ltd, shared that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (U.S. FDA) has given the green light to Jobevne™ (bevacizumab-nwgd), a biosimilar. This approval marks a significant milestone for the company and allows for the distribution of Jobevne™ in the U.S. market. With Biocon Ltd being listed on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE code: 532523) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE: BIOCON), this development is expected to have a positive impact on its financial performance. The successful approval of Jobevne™ demonstrates Biocon's commitment to providing high-quality biosimilars to cater to the healthcare needs of patients.
news.headline: Biocon Biologics receives FDA approval for Jobevne™, biosimilar bevacizumab, expanding oncology portfolio. 532523, NSE: BIOCON.
